scope: Convert spec-generator agent to a skill with bundled schema + deterministic frontmatter validator

behavioral_change_under_test.claim: The spec-generator now emits frontmatter whose enum values are guaranteed in-range, closing the drift that drew 9 schema-violation threads on PR #1995 and recurred on PR #1989.
behavioral_change_under_test.evidence: uv run pytest .claude/skills/spec-generator/tests/test_validate_spec_frontmatter.py -> 20 passed. Negative cases pin the exact documented drift: priority=medium rejected, category=tooling rejected, task status=ready rejected, design missing status+priority rejected, complexity out-of-range rejected.
behavioral_change_under_test.why_deterministic_beats_llm_judge: Frontmatter enum membership is a closed, decidable property. A validator that mirrors the schema enums verbatim (.claude/skills/spec-generator/scripts/validate_spec_frontmatter.py, canonical-source-mirror compliant) proves the property for every input class; an LLM judge would sample it stochastically and could pass an invalid case by luck. The skill makes running this validator a BLOCKING gate, so the property holds at generation time, not just in tests.

structural_changes.rationale: The remaining edits are routing renames with no semantic change to spec-authoring logic, which moved verbatim from the agent body into the skill.

per_change..claude/commands/spec.md: Step 6 invocation changed from Task(subagent_type=spec-generator) to Skill(skill=spec-generator). Same downstream artifacts; the skill additionally self-validates.
per_change.templates/agents/orchestrator.shared.md: Removed spec-generator from the agent-delegation table (it is no longer an agent) and updated the lifecycle routing line to name the /spec skill. No change to how any retained agent is selected.
per_change..claude/skills/requirements-interview/SK: Handoff reference repointed from .claude/agents/spec-generator.md (removed) to .claude/skills/spec-generator/ (new). Documentation-only.
per_change..claude/skills/panning-for-gold/SKILL.md: Cross-reference reworded from 'spec-generator agent' to 'spec-generator skill'. Documentation-only.

why_no_llm_eval: ADR-057 behavioral eval is for changes that alter how the model interprets a prompt or workflow.
These edits are Task->Skill invocation renames and a table-row removal; the spec-authoring instructions are byte-identical in their new home.
scripts/eval/eval-prompt-change.py has no scenario that exercises the changed lines; a fabricated scenario would measure the unchanged authoring logic, not the rename.

manual_review.reviewed_by: agent + deterministic contract test + structural diff inspection
manual_review.follow_up: If a future change touches the spec-authoring logic itself (EARS rules, tier handling), run a live before/after eval on the SKILL.md with a generation scenario.
